Fabiana, thanks for calling. We have a serious disruption. The backlog is growing every day.
I know. The carrier should have confirmed the clearance last week. What happened?
They must have submitted the old forms. It was an oversight in the handover process.
We should have flagged this escalation earlier. Can we expedite the process?
Yes. I have already activated the contingency plan. But we need to resolve it before the deadline or we face a penalty.
I will account for the delay with the client. The disruption might have been avoided if we had automated alerts. Let me bring that proposal to the team.

We need to account for the delay and activate the contingency plan.

Hi Fabiana, it is Marcus again. We have a serious situation with the APAC terminal shipment. The carrier should have confirmed the customs clearance last Friday, but they did not. As a result, we now have a backlog of thirty units sitting at the port. The bottleneck is in the documentation process. Our logistics team must have missed the updated compliance requirements. I think we should have flagged this escalation earlier. The disruption might have been avoided if we had implemented a contingency plan. Can we schedule an urgent call? We need to expedite the clearance before the deadline on Thursday, or we will face a penalty from the client. The inventory is running low at the Tokyo office.

Good afternoon everyone. Quick update on the APAC shipment disruption. After reviewing the situation, it is clear that we should have caught the documentation issue earlier. The bottleneck was caused by an oversight in our compliance process. The carrier must have submitted the old forms instead of the updated ones. We have now expedited the customs clearance and expect the backlog to be resolved by Friday. To prevent future disruptions, I recommend we phase out our current manual tracking system and implement an automated contingency alert. The escalation could have been avoided entirely if we had flagged the deadline conflict two weeks ago. The penalty clause has been waived because we accounted for the delay transparently with the client. Inventory levels should normalize by next Wednesday.

Marcus reports a serious shipment disruption. The carrier missed the customs clearance deadline. A backlog of thirty units is sitting at the port. Fabiana escalates the issue and expedites the process. The team implements a contingency plan to prevent future disruptions.
